prompt: What is something small that brought you comfort today?
Who in your life are you quietly thankful for, and why?
Describe a moment from the past week that you want to remember.
What is something you believed a year ago that you no longer do?
Where are you being too hard on yourself?
What would you do differently if you weren't afraid of failing?
What has been weighing on your mind lately, and why?
When did you last feel truly at peace? What were the conditions?
What does your ideal ordinary day look like?
If your current mood were a weather pattern, what would it be?
Describe your life right now as if it were the opening line of a novel.
What color best represents how you feel this week, and why?
What do you wish someone in your life understood about you?
Describe a conversation you keep putting off. What's stopping you?
Who do you show up best for, and what does that reveal about you?
What sensations are you aware of in your body right now?
What thought keeps returning to you today? Just notice it — don't judge it.
What would it feel like to let go of one expectation you're holding?
What was I worried about that turned out not to mater?
Where have I grown in ways I didn't notice while it was happening?
What old questions still feel alive and deserve more time?
What am I carrying that I haven't said out loud to anyone?
What emotion have I been avoiding? What if I turned towards it?
When did I last feel genuinely at peace? What was happening?
What does my inner critic say most often? Is any of it true?
What am I grieving, even queitly or something small?
What would I feel if I stopped trying to fix myself for one day?
When do I feel most like myself? Least like it?
If my emotions today were weather, what is the forecast?
What do I need right now that I am not giving myself?
What is the kindest thing I could say to myself today/right now?
What would I tell a close friend feeling exactly this?
What is one true thing I can say about just this moment?
What has survived in me that I thought wouldn't?
If this difficulty were a teacher, what is it showing me?
What would 'enough' look like today? Not perfect, just enough.
Did today reflect what I say I value?
Where did I take the easy path when I knew there was a harder, more honest one?
What small thing happened today that I want to remember?
What am I trying to build, and who is it actually for?
What would I research or teach if nobody was evaluating it?
Where do my academic interests and my personal beliefs feel in tension?
What does a fair world actually look like in concrete terms, not abstractly but in daily life?
Where do I see my values reflected in the world around me, even in small ways?
What belief have I held for a long time that I'm starting to question?
What specifically am I angry at today? What is the anger telling me?
Where is the line between righteous frustration and consuming resentment?
Who do I find it hardest to extend understanding to and why?
What did I read that stopped me? What was it actually saying?
Where did I notice a gap today between how I want to move through the world and how I actually did?
What would it look like to fully accept this moment without abandoning the desire to change things?
What do I actually believe happens after we dies, not what I'm supposed to believe?
What would I do differently if I had one year left to live?
What does a good life look lide, specifically?
What is the difference between happiness and meaning? Which do I want?
What do I think the purpose of suffering is?
What does freedom mean tom me and am I living it?
What is the relationship between love and fear in how I live?
What would it mean to fully accept that everyything is impermanent?
Is there a gap between who I am and who I want to be?
If I could ask one question and receive a completely honest answer, what would it be?
What do I believe about God, the sacred, or the largest thing I could imagine?
What moment of beauty stopped me this week, even briefly?
When did I last feel genuine wonder and what produced it?
What would I create if I knew no one would ever see it?
What does the natural world tell me that people don't?
What story am I telling about my life? IS it the truest version?
If I wrote a poem right now, even a terrible one, what would it be about?
What has sitting taught me that I couldn't learn any other way?
When my mind wonders, where does it go and what does that tell me?
Where is the cushion leaking into daily life, even in small ways?
What teaching from my reading has stayed with me this week?
What does self-compassion actually feel like when I manage it?
If my practice were a living thing, is it a seedling, sapling, or oak?
What is the most uncomfortable thing practice has shown me about myself?
